channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. military says it shot down Iranian drones launched toward Gulf allies
item.description: The exchange of strikes comes as the Trump administration ramps up pressure on Iran to make a deal to end the conflict.
item.content:encoded:
The exchange of strikes comes as the Trump administration ramps up pressure on Iran to make a deal to end the conflict.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Putin promotes a new world economic order at St. Petersburg forum
item.description: At "Russian Davos," Putin ruled out meeting with Zelenskyy and promoted a new world economic order.
item.content:encoded: At "Russian Davos," Putin ruled out meeting with Zelenskyy and promoted a new world economic order.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: Xi Jinping will travel to North Korea next week in first visit since 2019
item.description: The announcement was made by both countries Friday a day after North Korea unveiled a new facility to produce nuclear fuel.
item.content:encoded:
The announcement was made by both countries Friday a day after North Korea unveiled a new facility to produce nuclear fuel.

(Image credit: Ahn Young-joon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: South Africa rolls out game-changing HIV shot amid funding shortfalls
item.description: A new twice-yearly HIV prevention injection could transform South Africa's fight against the epidemic — but U.S. aid cuts and limited doses threaten to slow its impact.
item.content:encoded:
A new twice-yearly HIV prevention injection could transform South Africa's fight against the epidemic — but U.S. aid cuts and limited doses threaten to slow its impact.

(Image credit: Ihsaan Haffe)
item.dc:creator: Kate Bartlett

item.title: China's highest bridge brings tourists and internet access to remote communities
item.description: A link to the highway that cuts travel times from hours to just minutes, and a symbol of a flow of investment that has provided unprecedented access to high-speed internet in this remote region.
item.content:encoded:
A link to the highway that cuts travel times from hours to just minutes, and a symbol of a flow of investment that has provided unprecedented access to high-speed internet in this remote region.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Protesters in Albania oppose plan for Trump family-linked resort
item.description: Protesters in Albania oppose Jared Kushner's planned Adriatic coast resort, which would be located in one of the Mediterranean's most environmentally sensitive areas.
item.content:encoded: Protesters in Albania oppose Jared Kushner's planned Adriatic coast resort, which would be located in one of the Mediterranean's most environmentally sensitive areas.
item.dc:creator: Megan Williams

item.title: Putin says Russia will bolster air defenses in response to Ukrainian drone attacks
item.description: President Vladimir Putin says Russia will strengthen its air defenses to counter recent Ukrainian drone attacks, which have reached deep inside his country and cast a cloud over his showcase economic forum in his hometown of St. Petersburg.
item.content:encoded:
President Vladimir Putin says Russia will strengthen its air defenses to counter recent Ukrainian drone attacks, which have reached deep inside his country and cast a cloud over his showcase economic forum in his hometown of St. Petersburg.

(Image credit: Dmitri Lovetsky)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: North Korea unveils a new plant to produce fuel for nuclear weapons
item.description: State media photos on the place showed it is likely a plant to produce weapons-grade uranium.
item.content:encoded:
State media photos on the place showed it is likely a plant to produce weapons-grade uranium.

(Image credit: Korean Central News Agency)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Hezbollah rejects ceasefire deal agreed on by Israel and Lebanon
item.description: The U.N. peacekeeping mission for Lebanon, known as UNIFIL, said one peacekeeper was killed and others were wounded when they came under mortar fire in southeastern Lebanon.
item.content:encoded:
The U.N. peacekeeping mission for Lebanon, known as UNIFIL, said one peacekeeper was killed and others were wounded when they came under mortar fire in southeastern Lebanon.

(Image credit: Jalaa Marey)
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Russia's economic forum opens against backdrop of Ukrainian strikes
item.description: The St. Petersburg International Economic Forum -- Russia's answer to Davos -- opens under the shadow of Ukrainian drone attacks and growing economic uncertainty.
item.content:encoded: The St. Petersburg International Economic Forum -- Russia's answer to Davos -- opens under the shadow of Ukrainian drone attacks and growing economic uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes
